author: draganrakita
content: Have made PR that introduces runtime cost and splits previous gas function intro intrinsic and runtime: https://github.com/ethereum/EIPs/pull/11844 pls take a look

author: misilva73
content: Left a couple comments. The main one is about the addition of the WARM_COST pre-charge. I am not sure I see the point in that. That will then chnage a lot of the remaining changes, so I wnated to settle that one before sontinuing the review

author: draganrakita
content:
I wanted to be accurate about it, and the gas for warm/cold is considered. This means that if target/to address is already in auth_list/access_list we will not charge additional cost for warming. 

Initial version of eip2780 did this as part of intrinsic gas this meant fetching state and doing this inside txpool, if it is part of runtime it looked okay to me. We already need to add cold/warm cost if target/to is delegate.

author: misilva73
content:
> Initial version of eip2780 did this as part of intrinsic gas
Did it? This was not how the EIP was drafted, right?

I think there is a simplicity argument for doing it simpler for the auth_list/access_list. Both mechanisms are not that much used, so I would rather that just everything as cold

author: gurukamath
content: I think we should always charge cold access for recipient in `intrinsic` and warm/cold (as applicable) for delegation target during `runtime`

author: gurukamath
content: Adding a cold `to` surcharge in run-time seems too complex without much payoff

author: draganrakita
content: From [here](https://github.com/ethereum/EIPs/blob/75e3e82c22f30dc8465ced0bbbf984b4f03e8667/EIPS/eip-2780.md)  `CALL warms its to after charging the appropriate cold cost if cold.` and even there was case if `tx.to` is precompile

author: draganrakita
content: At this point, i dont mind it, it is more accurate to charge warm/cold even for `tx.to` but it is not something that matters a lot to me.

author: gurukamath
content: Just want to confirm. This comment still holds right? https://discord.com/channels/595666850260713488/1504670887851327648/1509909296471736472

author: gurukamath
content: Cause if it isn't, we can just charge warm/cold recipient at the intrinsic level itself

author: draganrakita
content: This comment was about intrinsic gas and additional fetch inside txpool. With runtime gas I am fine to be more precise.

author: misilva73
content: You are using an old version of the EIP. I don't think we want to do this

author: chfast
content: Have you discussed the clash with EIP-7623? I.e. what is `TX_BASE` now?

author: draganrakita
content: We didn't, will add note about it in EIP-2780, `TX_BASE_COST` is `12,000` rn.

author: chfast
content: Thanks. Back to messing with 8037 then...

author: gurukamath
content:
In that case, I'd propose the following
1. transaction recipient - charge cold access at intrinsic level. (no warm at intrinsic + surcharge at run-time)
2. Type 4 transaction auth tuple authority - charge cold access at intrinsic level. (no warm at intrinsic + surcharge at run-time) => `REGULAR_PER_AUTH_BASE_COST = 7816`
3. delegation target of the transaction recipient - no charge at intrinsic; charge warm/cold (as applicable) at run-time

author: mariusvanderwijden
content:
You're coming from this from a different angle than us.
We figured out today 🙈  that in the current spec we are not using LIFO  but refund straight to the state gas.
This is (afaiu) only necessary because of this.
I would like to get to a world where we either
(1) Don't refund state gas in the outer tx frame
(2) Use LIFO when returning state gas in the outer tx frame

author: draganrakita
content:
I think this is really good summary! I wouldn't mind it, but still prefer more granular thing that is in RP.

Would like to see what other clients think about it (dont want to tag all teams, but <@&1460290804898861117> tag looks okay)

author: garyrong
content: But the state gas refund is inevitable even with dragan's proposal. One example is for creation-tx, it's always possible the creation frame will fail and the pre-charged state cost has to be refunded

author: draganrakita
content: That looks fine, as it aligned with what sub frame would do.

author: garyrong
content: Yeah I am replying to Marius. (1) is not possible
